food_dishes.name: Tender Coconut Water
food_dishes.description: The local variety — shorter, harder, sweeter — freshly cut on beach and roadside stalls

food_dishes.name: Solkadhi
food_dishes.description: Kokum and coconut milk digestive drink — essential after heavy seafood meal, cooling
food_dishes.best_where: With fish meals at Konkani restaurants

food_dishes.name: Ideal Ice Cream (Cashew/Sitaphal)
food_dishes.description: Mangalore-institution since 1975 — cashew praline and custard apple flavours, nothing like this elsewhere

food_dishes.name: Kaju Katli (Cashew Barfi)
food_dishes.description: Mangalore is a major cashew processing hub — freshest cashew sweets available here directly from processors

food_dishes.name: Halwa (Mangalore style)
food_dishes.description: Coconut milk halwa — translucent, slightly chewy, distinctly different from other Indian halwas

food_dishes.name: Sorpotel
food_dishes.description: Pork offal slow-cooked in vinegar and spices — Portuguese-influenced, served at Christmas and feasts

food_dishes.name: Neer Dosa
food_dishes.description: Paper-thin rice crêpe made with soaked rice batter, no fermentation needed, served with coconut chutney or chicken curry

food_dishes.name: Idli Sambar (Udupi style)
food_dishes.description: Lighter, more sour than Bengaluru idli, served with thin watery sambar — a specific preparation

food_dishes.name: Pundi / Moode
food_dishes.description: Steamed rice dumplings in different shapes — cone (moode) or round (pundi), eaten with fish curry

food_dishes.name: Sannas
food_dishes.description: Steamed rice and coconut toddy cake — soft, slightly fermented, like a regional idli — Christmas staple

food_dishes.name: Mangalore Bun
food_dishes.description: Sweet deep-fried banana bread — unique to Mangalore, found only here, eaten with coconut chutney

food_dishes.name: Kane Fish Fry (Lady Fish)
food_dishes.description: Lady fish marinated in red masala and shallow-fried, a Mangalore signature — found nowhere else this way

food_dishes.name: Bangude Masala (Mackerel curry)
food_dishes.description: Mangalorean mackerel curry with kudampuli (Gambooge), coconut, and coastal spices

food_dishes.name: Prawn Masala / Gassi
food_dishes.description: Coastal prawn curry with thick coconut base and local spices — gassi is the quintessential Tulu curry

food_dishes.name: Pomfret Tawa Fry
food_dishes.description: Whole pomfret marinated in masala paste, tawa-fried with minimal oil — crispy skin, moist inside

food_dishes.name: Goli Baje
food_dishes.description: Soft deep-fried fritters of maida and curds, served with coconut chutney — the Mangalore answer to bhajias

food_dishes.name: Pathrode
food_dishes.description: Colocasia leaves stuffed with spiced rice paste and steamed or pan-fried — earthy, unique

food_dishes.name: Chicken Ghee Roast
food_dishes.description: Dry-cooked chicken in reduction of red chilli, tamarind and ghee — intensely flavoured, originated in Kundapur

food_dishes.name: Kori Rotti
food_dishes.description: Crispy wafer-thin rice roti soaked in chicken curry just before eating — Bunt community celebration dish

food_dishes.name: Beary Biryani
food_dishes.description: Distinct Beary Muslim biryani — shorter grain, coconut milk base, less spice than Hyderabadi, deeply aromatic

religious_spiritual.name: Shri Jnanashakthi Subrahmanyaswami Temple, Nagavruja Kshetra Pavanje

tags.description: ದಕ್ಷಿಣ ಕನ್ನಡ ಜಿಲ್ಲೆಯ ಹಳೆಯಂಗಡಿ ಸಮೀಪದ ಪಾವಂಜೆ ಕ್ಷೇತ್ರದಲ್ಲಿ ಶ್ರೀ ಜ್ಞಾನಶಕ್ತಿ ಸುಬ್ರಹ್ಮಣ್ಯಸ್ವಾಮಿ ದೇವಸ್ಥಾನವಿದೆ. ಪ್ರಾಚೀನ ಕಾಲದಿಂದಲೂ "ನಾಗವೃಜ ಕ್ಷೇತ್ರ"ವೆಂದೇ ಪ್ರಸಿದ್ಧಿ ಪಡೆದ ಈ ಪ್ರದೇಶದಲ್ಲಿ ಪಾವಂಜೆ ಕೋಡಿಮನೆ ವಾಸುಭಟ್ಟರು 1920ರಲ್ಲಿ ತಮ್ಮ ಮೂಲನಾಗ ಸನ್ನಿಧಿಯಲ್ಲಿ ಈ ದೇವಾಲಯವನ್ನು ಕಟ್ಟಿಸಿ
tags.name: Shri Jnanashakthi Subrahmanyaswami Temple, Nagavruja Kshetra Pavanje
tags.name:kn: ಶ್ರೀ ಜ್ಞಾನಶಕ್ತಿ ಸುಬ್ರಹ್ಮಣ್ಯಸ್ವಾಮಿ ದೇವಸ್ಥಾನ, ನಾಗವೃಜ ಕ್ಷೇತ್ರ ಪಾವಂಜೆ.

historical_events.event: Vijayanagara Empire period — Mangalore under Vijayanagara suzerainty, thriving port trade
Portuguese arrive — Mangalore becomes important Portuguese trading port, churches built
First Catholic diocese in Karnataka established at Mangalore
Hyder Ali captures Mangalore — Mysore Sultanate period begins
Sultan Battery built by Tipu Sultan as river watchtower and cannon position
Treaty of Mangalore signed between Tipu Sultan and British East India Company — historically significant
Tipu Sultan falls at Srirangapatna — Mangalore reverts to British control
Milagres Church rebuilt in present form — Catholic community flourishes
St Aloysius Chapel built and frescoed by Italian Jesuit Fr Antonio Moscheni — architectural landmark
Basel Mission Tile Works established — famous Mangalore tiles exported worldwide
New Mangalore Port becomes fully operational — transforms city economically
City renamed Mangaluru (official Kannada name) though Mangalore still widely used
Air India Express crash at Mangalore airport — tragic aviation history

community_data.name: Tulu community
community_data.notes: Dominant indigenous community of coastal Karnataka

community_data.notes: Goud Saraswat Brahmins — prominent in business and temples

community_data.name: Beary community
community_data.notes: Muslim trading community, distinct dialect between Tulu and Malayalam

community_data.name: Mogaveera community
community_data.notes: Traditional fishing community — the backbone of Mangalore's seafood economy

community_data.name: Billava community
community_data.notes: Traditional toddy-tapper community, custodians of Paddana oral poetry

community_data.name: Catholic community
community_data.notes: Portuguese-influenced community, unique cuisine, famous choir traditions
